speaker: ANGELO
text: When I would pray and think, I think and pray

speaker: ANGELO
text: To several subjects. Heaven hath my empty words,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Whilst my invention, hearing not my tongue,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Anchors on Isabel. God in my mouth,

speaker: ANGELO
text: As if I did but only chew His name,

speaker: ANGELO
text: And in my heart the strong and swelling evil

speaker: ANGELO
text: Of my conception. The state whereon I studied

speaker: ANGELO
text: Is, like a good thing being often read,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Grown sere and tedious. Yea, my gravity,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Wherein—let no man hear me—I take pride,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Could I with boot change for an idle plume

speaker: ANGELO
text: Which the air beats for vain. O place, O form,

speaker: ANGELO
text: How often dost thou with thy case, thy habit,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Wrench awe from fools, and tie the wiser souls

speaker: ANGELO
text: To thy false seeming! Blood, thou art blood.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Let’s write “good angel” on the devil’s horn.

speaker: ANGELO
text:
’Tis not the devil’s crest.
                 
                Knock
                 
                within
                .
               How now, who’s there?

speaker: SERVANT
text: One Isabel, a sister, desires access to you.

speaker: ANGELO
text:
Teach her the way.
                 
                Servant
                 
                exits
                .
               O heavens,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Why does my blood thus muster to my heart,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Making both it unable for itself

speaker: ANGELO
text: And dispossessing all my other parts

speaker: ANGELO
text: Of necessary fitness?

speaker: ANGELO
text: So play the foolish throngs with one that swoons,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Come all to help him, and so stop the air

speaker: ANGELO
text: By which he should revive. And even so

speaker: ANGELO
text: The general subject to a well-wished king

speaker: ANGELO
text: Quit their own part, and in obsequious fondness

speaker: ANGELO
text: Crowd to his presence, where their untaught love

speaker: ANGELO
text: Must needs appear offense.

speaker: ANGELO
text: How now, fair maid?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I am come to know your pleasure.

speaker: ANGELO
text: That you might know it would much better please me

speaker: ANGELO
text: Than to demand what ’tis. Your brother cannot live.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Even so. Heaven keep your Honor.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Yet may he live a while. And it may be

speaker: ANGELO
text: As long as you or I. Yet he must die.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Under your sentence?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Yea.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: When, I beseech you? That in his reprieve,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Longer or shorter, he may be so fitted

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That his soul sicken not.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Ha! Fie, these filthy vices! It were as good

speaker: ANGELO
text: To pardon him that hath from nature stolen

speaker: ANGELO
text: A man already made, as to remit

speaker: ANGELO
text: Their saucy sweetness that do coin God’s image

speaker: ANGELO
text: In stamps that are forbid. ’Tis all as easy

speaker: ANGELO
text: Falsely to take away a life true made

speaker: ANGELO
text: As to put metal in restrainèd means

speaker: ANGELO
text: To make a false one.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: ’Tis set down so in heaven, but not in Earth.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Say you so? Then I shall pose you quickly:

speaker: ANGELO
text: Which had you rather, that the most just law

speaker: ANGELO
text: Now took your brother’s life, or, to redeem him,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Give up your body to such sweet uncleanness

speaker: ANGELO
text: As she that he hath stained?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Sir, believe this:

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I had rather give my body than my soul.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I talk not of your soul. Our compelled sins

speaker: ANGELO
text: Stand more for number than for accompt.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: How say you?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Nay, I’ll not warrant that, for I can speak

speaker: ANGELO
text: Against the thing I say. Answer to this:

speaker: ANGELO
text: I, now the voice of the recorded law,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Pronounce a sentence on your brother’s life.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Might there not be a charity in sin

speaker: ANGELO
text: To save this brother’s life?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Please you to do ’t,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I’ll take it as a peril to my soul,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: It is no sin at all, but charity.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Pleased you to do ’t, at peril of your soul,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Were equal poise of sin and charity.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That I do beg his life, if it be sin

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Heaven let me bear it. You granting of my suit,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: If that be sin, I’ll make it my morn prayer

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To have it added to the faults of mine

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And nothing of your answer.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Nay, but hear me.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Your sense pursues not mine. Either you are ignorant,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Or seem so, crafty, and that’s not good.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Let me be ignorant and in nothing good,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: But graciously to know I am no better.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Thus wisdom wishes to appear most bright

speaker: ANGELO
text: When it doth tax itself, as these black masks

speaker: ANGELO
text: Proclaim an enshield beauty ten times louder

speaker: ANGELO
text: Than beauty could, displayed. But mark me.

speaker: ANGELO
text: To be receivèd plain, I’ll speak more gross:

speaker: ANGELO
text: Your brother is to die.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: So.

speaker: ANGELO
text: And his offense is so, as it appears,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Accountant to the law upon that pain.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: True.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Admit no other way to save his life—

speaker: ANGELO
text: As I subscribe not that, nor any other—

speaker: ANGELO
text: But, in the loss of question, that you, his sister,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Finding yourself desired of such a person

speaker: ANGELO
text: Whose credit with the judge, or own great place,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Could fetch your brother from the manacles

speaker: ANGELO
text: Of the all-binding law, and that there were

speaker: ANGELO
text: No earthly mean to save him but that either

speaker: ANGELO
text: You must lay down the treasures of your body

speaker: ANGELO
text: To this supposed, or else to let him suffer,

speaker: ANGELO
text: What would you do?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As much for my poor brother as myself.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That is, were I under the terms of death,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Th’ impression of keen whips I’d wear as rubies

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And strip myself to death as to a bed

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That longing have been sick for, ere I’d yield

speaker: ISABELLA
text: My body up to shame.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Then must your brother die.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And ’twere the cheaper way.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Better it were a brother died at once

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Than that a sister, by redeeming him,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Should die forever.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Were not you then as cruel as the sentence

speaker: ANGELO
text: That you have slandered so?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Ignomy in ransom and free pardon

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Are of two houses. Lawful mercy

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Is nothing kin to foul redemption.

speaker: ANGELO
text: You seemed of late to make the law a tyrant,

speaker: ANGELO
text: And rather proved the sliding of your brother

speaker: ANGELO
text: A merriment than a vice.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O, pardon me, my lord. It oft falls out,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To have what we would have, we speak not what we mean.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I something do excuse the thing I hate

speaker: ISABELLA
text: For his advantage that I dearly love.

speaker: ANGELO
text: We are all frail.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Else let my brother die,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: If not a fedary but only he

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Owe and succeed thy weakness.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Nay, women are frail too.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Ay, as the glasses where they view themselves,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Which are as easy broke as they make forms.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Women—help, heaven—men their creation mar

speaker: ISABELLA
text: In profiting by them. Nay, call us ten times frail,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: For we are soft as our complexions are,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And credulous to false prints.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I think it well.

speaker: ANGELO
text: And from this testimony of your own sex,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Since I suppose we are made to be no stronger

speaker: ANGELO
text: Than faults may shake our frames, let me be bold.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I do arrest your words. Be that you are—

speaker: ANGELO
text: That is, a woman. If you be more, you’re none.

speaker: ANGELO
text: If you be one, as you are well expressed

speaker: ANGELO
text: By all external warrants, show it now

speaker: ANGELO
text: By putting on the destined livery.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I have no tongue but one. Gentle my lord,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Let me entreat you speak the former language.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Plainly conceive I love you.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: My brother did love Juliet,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And you tell me that he shall die for ’t.

speaker: ANGELO
text: He shall not, Isabel, if you give me love.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I know your virtue hath a license in ’t

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Which seems a little fouler than it is

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To pluck on others.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Believe me, on mine honor,

speaker: ANGELO
text: My words express my purpose.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Ha! Little honor to be much believed,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And most pernicious purpose. Seeming, seeming!

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I will proclaim thee, Angelo, look for ’t.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Sign me a present pardon for my brother

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Or with an outstretched throat I’ll tell the world aloud

speaker: ISABELLA
text: What man thou art.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Who will believe thee, Isabel?

speaker: ANGELO
text: My unsoiled name, th’ austereness of my life,

speaker: ANGELO
text: My vouch against you, and my place i’ th’ state

speaker: ANGELO
text: Will so your accusation overweigh

speaker: ANGELO
text: That you shall stifle in your own report

speaker: ANGELO
text: And smell of calumny. I have begun,

speaker: ANGELO
text: And now I give my sensual race the rein.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Fit thy consent to my sharp appetite;

speaker: ANGELO
text: Lay by all nicety and prolixious blushes

speaker: ANGELO
text: That banish what they sue for. Redeem thy brother

speaker: ANGELO
text: By yielding up thy body to my will,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Or else he must not only die the death,

speaker: ANGELO
text: But thy unkindness shall his death draw out

speaker: ANGELO
text: To ling’ring sufferance. Answer me tomorrow,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Or by the affection that now guides me most,

speaker: ANGELO
text: I’ll prove a tyrant to him. As for you,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Say what you can, my false o’erweighs your true.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To whom should I complain? Did I tell this,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Who would believe me? O, perilous mouths,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That bear in them one and the selfsame tongue,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Either of condemnation or approof,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Bidding the law make curtsy to their will,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Hooking both right and wrong to th’ appetite,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To follow as it draws. I’ll to my brother.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Though he hath fall’n by prompture of the blood,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Yet hath he in him such a mind of honor

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That, had he twenty heads to tender down

speaker: ISABELLA
text: On twenty bloody blocks, he’d yield them up

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Before his sister should her body stoop

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To such abhorred pollution.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Then, Isabel, live chaste, and, brother, die.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: More than our brother is our chastity.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I’ll tell him yet of Angelo’s request,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And fit his mind to death, for his soul’s rest.
